cards.definition: How full an anaerobic lagoon should be charged with cow dung slurry ahead of the season, to seed the bacterial culture that will treat the incoming effluent.
cards.analogy: A starter culture added before the main batch arrives, the way a sourdough starter needs to be alive before you bake with it.
cards.why_it_matters: Skipping this step means the lagoon isn't biologically ready when the first pulping effluent actually arrives.
cards.cloze: The anaerobic lagoon should be charged with cow dung slurry up to ___% of its capacity, one month before pulping starts.

cards.definition: How many days' worth of effluent production the anaerobic lagoon needs to be sized to hold, alongside its depth limit.
cards.analogy: A holding tank sized to a retention time, not just a volume — the effluent needs to sit long enough for treatment to actually happen.
cards.why_it_matters: Undersizing this is a common way an effluent system fails during a busy processing season, not at design time.
cards.cloze: The anaerobic lagoon's capacity should equal ___ days of effluent production, with a maximum depth of 3 metres.

cards.definition: The nitrogen (urea) dose added to the anaerobic lagoon per unit of effluent volume, to feed the bacterial digestion process.
cards.analogy: A nutrient supplement for the bacteria doing the actual treatment work, not for the coffee.
cards.why_it_matters: Getting this dose wrong under-feeds (slow treatment) or over-feeds (wasted input, possible imbalance) the lagoon's bacterial culture.
cards.cloze: For every 20,000 litres of effluent in the anaerobic lagoon, add ___ kg of urea.

cards.definition: The depth limit for the second-stage aerobic lagoon, which finishes treatment after the anaerobic stage — shallower than the anaerobic lagoon so oxygen can reach the whole depth.
cards.analogy: A shallow finishing pool rather than a deep holding tank, because this stage needs oxygen to reach the bottom.
cards.why_it_matters: The two-stage anaerobic-then-aerobic design only works if each stage is built to its own, different spec.
cards.cloze: The aerobic lagoon should have a depth of no more than ___ metre and capacity for 7 days of effluent production.
